title: Init npm project + Vite + CRXJS build tooling
details: package.json, vite.config with @crxjs/vite-plugin (fallback: esbuild script if CRXJS blocks). Output dist/ loadable unpacked. Add npm scripts: dev, build. Node 20+.

title: Write manifest.json (MV3, minimal permissions)
details: MV3. permissions: storage, contextMenus, scripting, activeTab. host_permissions: [] . default_locale: en. name/description via __MSG_ext_name__/__MSG_ext_description__. Placeholder icons. See SPEC §3 §7.

title: Scaffold 4 contexts: service worker, content script, popup, options
details: background/service-worker.js, content/main.js, popup/, options/ — each just logs a heartbeat and (for content) responds to a PING message from the worker. Verify message round-trip works.

title: Scaffold _locales for en, zh_CN, ms, ja, vi, ko
details: messages.json per locale with ext_name + ext_description. English text as placeholder in non-en files is OK at this stage. See SPEC §8 key conventions.

title: Lint/format config + CI workflow
details: ESLint + Prettier. GitHub Actions: install, lint, build on every PR. Fail on lint errors.

title: Selection detector in content script
details: Port REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §1 selection detector: mouseup+touchend debounced 250ms, selectionchange clears state, ignore <2 chars or collapsed, capture bounding rect. Emit internal event {text, rect}.

title: Shadow DOM UI host + trigger icon
details: One host element appended to documentElement, attachShadow, CSS reset inside. Floating icon appears near selection end on selection event; disappears on selection collapse/scroll. Must not steal focus or break copy.

title: Translation modal box (anchored) + dismissal
details: Click icon → modal anchored to selection rect (flip if near viewport edge). Shows source text + loading state + result area + Explain button (hidden for now). Dismiss: Esc, outside mousedown, × button. Port positioning from REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §7.

title: Mobile bottom-sheet variant + drag-to-dismiss
details: Viewport <640px: modal renders as bottom sheet, drag handle to dismiss (REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §7).

title: Context capture: surrounding paragraph
details: Walk up from selection commonAncestorContainer, take innerText, truncate ~1200 chars (REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §2). Sent along with translate request; needed later by Explain.

title: Message protocol + mock translator in service worker
details: DROPPED (user decision, 2026-07-03): skip the mock translator, go straight to real engines starting at T-013. The message protocol itself (MSG.TRANSLATE/EXPLAIN, {ok,data}/{ok,error} envelope) is already defined in shared/messages.js and documented in docs/ARCHITECTURE.md as part of T-001..T-010 wiring — only the mock-response part of this task is being skipped.

title: E2 exit-criteria test pass on 10 real sites
details: EXECUTED 2026-07-03 (same day as scaffolding): real Chrome, extension loaded unpacked via dist/, driven end-to-end over the Chrome DevTools Protocol against 10 live sites (real selection events, real clicks, real trial-gateway network calls — no mocking). 10/10 pass; 2 login-walled sites from the original list (x.com, Gmail) substituted with reachable public equivalents since no test credentials were available — documented transparently in docs/qa/e2-checklist.md rather than silently dropped. All testable cross-cutting checks pass (<2-char gating, scroll-hide, collapse-hide, mobile bottom-sheet with a real window resize, drag-to-dismiss, Explain end-to-end with a real graded bilingual payload). Zero extension-caused console errors across all sites. Two checks are structurally untestable via CDP automation (native context-menu click, OS clipboard) and verified by code inspection instead; trial-quota-exhaustion upsell deliberately not forced, per the standing T-021 owner decision. FOUND AND FIXED 1 blocking bug (post-report, from an owner screenshot the JSON-only assertions had missed): modal.js's `.modal-upsell-actions { display: flex }` unconditionally overrode the `hidden` attribute set after every successful translation (author CSS beats the UA default `[hidden]` rule at equal specificity), so two blank, unwired upsell buttons rendered under the Explain button on every single successful translation. Fixed with `.modal-upsell-actions[hidden] { display: none; }`; rebuilt, reloaded live, re-verified on the exact site/selection from the screenshot — bars gone. FOUND AND FIXED a 2nd blocking bug (also post-report, from another owner screenshot): selecting text near the bottom of the viewport could grow the modal (translation result, then Explain payload arriving after initial positioning) past the viewport edge with no way to reach the overflow — position() only measured/placed the box once while still small, and .modal had no max-height/overflow-y of its own. Fixed by capping .modal to the viewport (CSS max-height+overflow-y as a passive net) and having position() set an inline max-height bounded to the room actually available below the box's chosen top (no floor, so it never overflows even in the worst case) — growth now scrolls inside the box. Verified: reproduced (336px overflow, content genuinely unreachable) before the fix, confirmed 0px overflow + working internal scroll surfacing the previously-hidden content after; regression-checked a normal selection still positions correctly. One non-blocking polish item remains: modal chrome doesn't mirror for RTL host pages. Full per-site results + both bug writeups in docs/qa/e2-checklist.md. Reviewed and confirmed by owner 2026-07-03 — unblocks T-032.

title: Engine adapter interface + registry
details: interface: translate(text, targetLang, opts) / explain(...) / capabilities() / isAvailable(). Registry picks active engine from settings. All in service worker. See docs/ENGINES.md. Depends only on the worker skeleton (T-003) existing — T-011 (mock translator) was dropped; the message protocol it would have formalized is already defined in shared/messages.js + docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

title: Trial gateway adapter (REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §3)
details: Port REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §3: XOR decryptKey (seed 20260515) + SSE streaming parse for /v1/responses, model gpt-5.4-mini, reasoning.effort low. Bundled cipher string only — plaintext key NEVER in repo. AbortSignal timeout 30s.

title: Chrome on-device Translator adapter
details: Feature-detect Translator + LanguageDetector APIs (Chrome 138+). Handle model-download progress state. capabilities(): translate only, no explain. Graceful unavailable state.

title: BYOK adapter #1: Gemini
details: generateContent REST with user key from chrome.storage.local. Then clone pattern for OpenAI (T-017) and DeepSeek (T-018).

title: BYOK adapter #2: OpenAI
details: chat.completions or responses API with user key.

title: BYOK adapter #3: DeepSeek
details: OpenAI-compatible endpoint with user key.

title: Options page: engine, keys, target language, UI language
details: Engine picker, per-provider key fields (masked, stored chrome.storage.local), target-language default, UI-language note. All strings via chrome.i18n.

title: IndexedDB translation cache in service worker
details: Key: v{PROMPT_VERSION}::{targetLang}::{engine}::{sha256(text) short}. LRU cap. Version-bump invalidation per REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §6. See SPEC §6.

title: Probe live gateway quota-error shape + central error mapper
details: Deliberately exhausting/probing the live gateway daily limit was descoped per owner instruction (2026-07-03) — the gateway already has quota-handling logic server-side and forcing it isn't this project's job; the only other tester (the owner) confirmed no other users are on it yet, so there was no shared-impact concern, but triggering it was explicitly waved off anyway. Built the central error mapper instead (background/error-mapper.js): mapHttpError({status,bodyMessage,providerName,isTrialGateway}) + mapNetworkError + extractErrorMessage. All 4 engines (trial-gateway/gemini/openai/deepseek) refactored to call it instead of each duplicating status-code judgment calls. isTrialGateway:true (trial-gateway.js only) maps 429->trial_quota_exhausted; BYOK engines map 429->plain quota. Classification is interim/best-effort by standard HTTP semantics (401/403 auth, 429 quota-or-trial_quota_exhausted, else gateway_error) since the live shape was never confirmed — flagged clearly in docs/ENGINES.md as the one thing to revisit if the live gateway differs from standard HTTP conventions.

title: Trial-quota BYOK upsell UI
details: On trial_quota_exhausted: modal shows upsell (set up own key → opens options; try again tomorrow; on-device fallback if available). BYOK users' own quota errors show normal message, NOT upsell. See SPEC §4 §9.

title: Confirm cross-engine integration (re-scoped from "replace mock")
details: RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03: original title/scope ("replace mock") is stale — T-011 (mock translator) was dropped before any mock ever existed, and real engine results have been live in the modal since T-014. "Modal translate path uses active engine + cache" has been true since T-020; the trial-quota upsell path (T-022) already exercises this end-to-end with mocked responses. What is NOT yet verified: a holistic pass with all 5 REAL registered adapters (trial-gateway/on-device/gemini/openai/deepseek) together — switching engineId across all of them, confirming GET_CAPABILITIES reflects each switch, and confirming cache isolation holds across all 5, not just the fake-engine case tested in T-020. "Streaming: render tokens incrementally when engine supports SSE" is DEFERRED to ROADMAP v1.0 (already implied by its existing Performance bullet "modal open->first translated token < 1.5s") — SSE today is a transport-layer necessity (trial-gateway.js avoids Cloudflare edge timeout) but the modal only ever renders the fully-assembled result, no incremental UI. Not needed for MVP: translations are short selected phrases, not long documents (full-page translation is explicitly out of scope per SPEC §10), so the latency benefit of streaming UI is marginal for the actual use case.

title: Explain prompt + strict-JSON schema + loose parser
details: Port REFERENCE-SNIPPETS §5: schemaVersion, buildPrompt (CEFR-graded examples, simpler-vocab rule), parseJsonLoose fallback. LLM engines only.

title: Explain rendering in modal
details: Render explain payload: headword + POS/CEFR badges, dual-language definitions, graded examples, collapsible collocations/word-family/synonyms/antonyms/memory-tip. Escape all HTML. Explain button shown unconditionally after any successful translation (capability gating deferred to T-026, per its own scope) except the trial-quota upsell's on-device retry, where the engine is deterministically known not to support explain.

title: Explain cache + engine-capability gating
details: Cache key: explain::v{SCHEMA_VERSION}::{origin}::{normalized phrase}::{targetLang}. Hide/disable Explain button when active engine lacks explain capability, with i18n hint. Implemented as background/explain-cache.js (IndexedDB/LRU, mirrors translation-cache.js) wired into registry.js's explain(); main.js queries GET_CAPABILITIES before offering the button and shows it disabled (not hidden) with an i18n hint when canExplain is false, failing closed on error.

title: i18n completeness: translate all 6 locales for real
details: RE-SCOPED 2026-07-03: every task that added an i18n key (T-018, T-020..T-022, T-025, T-026) wrote a real translation directly into all 6 locale files as it went, rather than English placeholders — confirmed no commit ever carried the docs/I18N.md 'i18n-placeholder' tag. Verified 2026-07-03: all 6 locales have identical 46-key sets; the only value identical across every locale is ext_name (the brand name, deliberately kept as-is). REMAINING GAP (owner-acknowledged, not blocking): every non-English string is LLM-translated only — no native-speaker human review pass has happened yet for zh_CN/ja/ko/ms/vi. Tracked as a pre-launch follow-up, revisit before T-031 (store listing) / T-032 (submission).

title: CI i18n check: no hardcoded strings, no missing keys
details: Script: every getMessage key exists in all 6 messages.json; grep heuristic for hardcoded UI literals in content/popup/options. Wire into CI. Implemented as scripts/check-i18n.mjs (npm run lint:i18n, wired into .github/workflows/ci.yml): checks key parity across all 6 locales, flags keys referenced in code/manifest/HTML but undefined in en (typo detection), flags en keys never referenced (warning only), and a grep heuristic (2+ alphabetic words) over content/popup/options JS string literals + HTML text nodes lacking data-i18n, with a `// i18n-ok` suppression escape hatch. Fixed a real gap it found: explain_section_* keys (T-025) didn't match any documented I18N.md prefix — added explain_section_ to the table and the checker's allowlist. Manually verified by injecting one violation of each of the 4 kinds and confirming each was caught, then reverting.

title: Context menu: Translate selection
details: contextMenus entry (i18n title) → same pipeline as icon click. Implemented: service-worker.js creates the 'translate-selection' item (contexts: ['selection']) idempotently in onInstalled (removeAll then create, so a re-fired onInstalled never throws on a duplicate id); its onClicked handler sends a new worker→content MENU_TRANSLATE_SELECTION message via chrome.tabs.sendMessage (targeted at just that tab, not the extension-wide broadcast) carrying info.selectionText. main.js's new onMessage listener re-derives the live selection rect and calls the same translateSelection() the trigger icon uses. Incidental cleanup: removed the OPEN_OPTIONS message type from shared/messages.js — it was dead code (both popup.js and main.js already called chrome.runtime.openOptionsPage() directly, never through a message).

title: Icons + branding
details: 16/32/48/128 px extension icons, trigger-icon glyph, consistent palette. Update manifest. Implemented: public/icons/icon-{16,32,48,128}.png reuse the exact glyph already drawn in src/content/trigger-icon.js's ICON_SVG (the 'A' + CJK-character translate motif) on the same #2563eb brand blue circle, so the toolbar icon and the in-page trigger icon are visually the same mark — the trigger-icon glyph itself already existed from T-007, so this task's real work was rasterizing it to the 4 required PNG sizes and wiring manifest.json's icons + action.default_icon fields. Icons generated via an SVG->canvas->PNG rasterization in a real browser (not a static asset pipeline dependency) and verified by decoding each file back via Image() in-browser to confirm valid, correctly-sized PNGs. No new palette needed — #2563eb was already the established primary color across trigger-icon.js/modal.js.

title: Privacy policy + Web Store listing (6 locales)
details: State exactly what text is sent to which engine and when; zero-retention claims only where true. Store listing copy in all 6 languages. Implemented under docs/store/: PRIVACY-POLICY.md (English canonical, verified per-engine data flow against the real code — trial-gateway/on-device/gemini/openai/deepseek — confirmed with the owner 2026-07-03 that the trial gateway tracks usage counts only, not request/response content, so the 'not stored' claim is accurate) + privacy-policy-<locale>.md full translations for zh_CN/ja/ko/ms/vi; listing-<locale>.md (title/summary/description) for all 6 locales; SUBMISSION-NOTES.md for the Developer Dashboard fields that aren't locale-specific (single purpose, permission justifications, data-usage disclosure — English only, that's how the Dashboard works). Incidental fix discovered while justifying permissions: manifest.json's 'scripting' and 'activeTab' were requested but never used anywhere in the code — removed (owner-approved) in the same change; SPEC.md/CLAUDE.md updated to match. Publishing the policy to a real public URL and capturing store screenshots are manual steps still owed before actual submission (T-032).

title: MVP end-to-end QA + store submission
details: QA PORTION DONE 2026-07-03: closed both gaps T-012 had flagged as code-review-only. (1) Context menu: fired the exact MENU_TRANSLATE_SELECTION message chrome.tabs.sendMessage sends, live from the service worker's own execution context (not just reading the code) — modal opened and translated correctly, genuine end-to-end proof. (2) Clipboard: confirmed inconclusive by design — document.execCommand('copy') requires a real user gesture CDP-triggered JS can't provide, so it fails identically on any site with or without the extension; falls back to the existing code-inspection finding (trigger-icon.js's preventDefault/stopPropagation is scoped only to the icon itself). BYOK engine-switch verified genuinely end-to-end: saved a (deliberately invalid, no real credentials available) Gemini key via the options UI, switched engineId, confirmed both persisted across a fresh page load (EPICS E3's 'survives restart' criterion), then triggered a real translation — Gemini's real API rejected the dummy key with a real 'API key not valid' auth error that the error-mapper surfaced cleanly in the modal (proves the adapter genuinely calls out with the stored key, not a silent no-op; a real working key would need the owner's own credentials to go further). Trial-quota upsell: left as covered only by T-022's original mocked-response test, per the same standing T-021 decision not to force real gateway exhaustion. Captured 2 real 1280×800 screenshots (translate result + Explain payload) for the store listing — draft quality, in session scratchpad, owner should pick/polish final ones. BLOCKED on the submission portion: actually publishing needs the owner's own Chrome Web Store Developer Dashboard account (one-time $5 fee if not already registered) and a real public URL for PRIVACY-POLICY.md (SUBMISSION-NOTES.md flagged this is still unpublished) — both require the owner directly, not something to do autonomously. Once the owner has those two things, packaging dist/ and walking the Dashboard fields (already drafted in docs/store/) is quick.

title: Popup: settings entry point + live engine status
details: Added mid-M3 per direct user request (2026-07-03) after seeing the M1 placeholder popup — not originally scoped as its own task. Popup shows the currently active engine (via GET_CAPABILITIES, friendly name from a shared engine-label map) and a Settings button that opens the options page, plus a one-line usage hint for first-time users. Replaces the M1 popup_status_ready placeholder.

title: Modal: manual resize via drag handles (desktop)
details: Added 2026-07-03 per direct user request after T-012's viewport-overflow fix, to let users proactively size the box instead of relying only on auto-fit/internal scroll. Desktop only (SPEC updated): 4 edge handles (top/right/bottom/left), min 260x160px, can't drag past the viewport edge (reuses the same EDGE_MARGIN clamp position() already uses). Implemented as a DOM/CSS split — .modal (outer: position/size/shadow/radius, overflow:visible so the handles, deliberately positioned straddling the edge, stay hit-testable) wrapping a new .modal-content (inner: padding/overflow-y:auto/radius:inherit) that now holds everything previously appended straight to .modal. This split was necessary because .modal's own overflow-y:auto (from the earlier viewport-overflow fix) implicitly clips overflow-x too, which silently ate the resize handles on first attempt — caught by live CDP testing, not by reading the code. Size persists across translations on the same page (inline style survives) and across page loads/sessions via chrome.storage.local ('modalSize', read/written only in main.js — modal.js stays chrome-API-free per its existing design note). Mobile is-sheet mode explicitly resets any inline width/height/left left over from a prior desktop resize (same class of bug as the upsell-buttons hidden-attribute fix: inline styles beat class CSS regardless of viewport) and hides the resize handles via CSS. Verified live: dragged all 3 non-corner-redundant edges (right +150px, bottom +100px, left -80px correctly clamped at the edge margin), confirmed persistence in chrome.storage.local and restoration on a fresh page navigation, and confirmed mobile mode still gets the drag-to-dismiss handle instead with resize handles display:none. FOLLOW-UP (same day): the handles had zero visual affordance — cursor: *-resize alone with no on-box visual cue is easy to miss, so a user might never discover the box is resizable. Added a short ::before grip line (brand blue, 3px, centered on each edge) that fades in on hover and stays lit for the whole drag via a JS-toggled .is-active class (CSS :hover alone flickers off once the pointer leaves the thin 8px strip mid-drag, even though the resize itself keeps tracking correctly via window-level listeners). Verified live via a real CDP Input.dispatchMouseEvent hover (not just reading the CSS) — element.matches(':hover') true, ::before opacity 0.55, visible in a screenshot as a subtle blue line at the hovered edge. 2ND FOLLOW-UP (same day, from an owner screenshot on a real daily-driver Chrome): dragging the height taller was a FIXED size, not a cap — resizing once for a long Explain payload then left a large permanent blank gap under every later, shorter translation for the rest of the page session, since box.style.height stayed pinned to the dragged value regardless of new content. Changed height (only height, not width — extra width isn't visually wasteful the way blank vertical space is) to CAP semantics: onEnd() for top/bottom edges now clears the explicit height and stores the dragged value as userHeightCap instead, applied through position()'s existing max-height mechanism (min of the viewport-derived cap and userHeightCap). The box now shrinks to fit whatever content it currently has and grows back up to (never past) the user's chosen cap when a later Explain payload needs the room, scrolling beyond that via the existing overflow-y. Verified live end-to-end on one page (not across a navigation, to avoid a chrome.storage.local write/read race with a live-testing script's own timing): natural box 117px -> dragged bottom edge +250px -> cap correctly captured as 367px (117+250) -> a 2nd, different short translation on the same page correctly stayed at 117px (no blank gap) -> clicking Explain grew it back to exactly 367px (the cap), not fixed nor unbounded. 3RD FOLLOW-UP (same day, from another owner screenshot, real usage): content looked cut off at the top after switching translations. Two real, distinct bugs, not one: (1) resetExplainBody() cleared the explain payload's innerHTML/hidden state but never reset the SCROLL POSITION of .modal-content — since the box/content elements are reused (not recreated) across translations, scrolling down to read one long Explain payload left that scroll position in place for the NEXT, unrelated translation's fresh content, making it look like the top was missing rather than just scrolled past. Fixed by resetting contentEl.scrollTop = 0 in both showModal() (every new translation) and showExplainResult() (every new explain payload). (2) A deeper, more serious latent bug surfaced while reproducing bug 1 with forced overflow: .modal-content's overflow containment had been silently broken since the .modal/.modal-content DOM split (this same task's original FOLLOW-UP entry) — .modal-content used height:100% to fill .modal, but .modal is only ever given a max-height (never an explicit height), and a max-height-clamped 'auto' height doesn't count as a "definite height" for a child's height:100% to resolve against per the CSS spec, so .modal-content silently ignored the cap and grew to fit ALL content unbounded, with .modal's overflow:visible (needed for the resize handles) then letting that excess spill outside the card boundary uncontained rather than being clipped or scrolled. This had been present since the resize-handle commits and was never re-verified afterward (the T-034 height-cap fix's own live test happened not to produce genuine overflow, so it passed without exercising this path). Fixed by giving .modal-content its OWN max-height directly (position()/wireResize now set contentEl.style.maxHeight instead of box.style.maxHeight; the CSS baseline moved from .modal to .modal-content too) instead of relying on percentage inheritance — .modal itself now has no height-related CSS at all, it just auto-wraps contentEl's (possibly capped) rendered size. Verified live end-to-end, real Explain payload, constrained viewport: modal height 367px (matching the stored userHeightCap from the previous verification), .modal-content scrollHeight 447 vs clientHeight 367, isScrollable true, content renders cleanly contained within the card with no overlap or spill, confirmed via screenshot. REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-04, full-file review of modal.js at owner request): fixed 2 more issues in this feature's territory. (a) .modal-explain-body still had its own max-height:320px + overflow-y from T-025 (needed back when nothing else scrolled) — now that .modal-content is the single scroll container, that inner cap made manual resize useless for exactly the content it exists for (dragging the box taller never revealed more Explain content, just added a second nested scrollbar). Removed; one scroll container now. Verified live: with a tall user cap + Explain open, explainBody scrollHeight == clientHeight (no inner scrollbar), computed max-height none. (b) top-edge drag release left the box's top pinned while the cap conversion shrank the height, so the bottom floated up away from the selection anchor — a top-edge drag anchors the BOTTOM; onEnd now re-derives top so the bottom stays put. Verified live: bottom 391.375 -> 391.375 (exact) across a +200px top-edge drag and release, height correctly back to content size. 4TH FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-04, from another owner screenshot, real usage): content visibly leaked past the box's rounded outline WHILE ACTIVELY DRAGGING the top/bottom handle to shrink it (not just a post-release state) — Explain/translation text rendered below the white box, overlapping the underlying page. Root cause: onMove (the real-time drag feedback loop) only ever updated box.style.height; contentEl.style.maxHeight (the actual clip boundary — .modal itself is deliberately overflow:visible so the resize handles stay hit-testable) was left untouched until onEnd. For the whole duration of a shrink-drag, contentEl kept clipping at its PRE-drag (larger) cap while box's own outline shrank to the new, smaller box.style.height in real time — since box has no clipping of its own, contentEl (now taller than its shrunk parent) spilled out through that overflow:visible for as long as the drag was in progress. Not a defect in the height-cap CONVERSION logic from the earlier follow-ups (that only runs on release, and still works correctly) — the two elements were simply unsynced for the whole drag, not just at its end. Fixed by mirroring contentEl.style.maxHeight to box.style.height inside onMove itself for both edges, so the clip boundary tracks the visual box size continuously through the entire drag. Verified live: grew the box tall with a real Explain payload, then re-dragged the bottom handle upward while sampling DOM state at each mid-drag step with the mouse still held down (before mouseup) — .modal-content's own bounding-box bottom matched .modal's shrinking bottom edge exactly at every sampled step (no escape), and a screenshot taken mid-drag shows the payload cleanly clipped inside the box with nothing spilling onto the page. Regression-checked qa-resize.mjs/qa-hover.mjs/qa-review-fixes2.mjs — all still pass.

title: Text-to-speech: speak buttons + per-language voice picker + auto-play
details: Added 2026-07-03 per direct user request, pulled forward from ROADMAP v1.x item #4 (originally scoped for after v1.0 hardening) — see ROADMAP.md. Free, no network call, no API key: uses the browser's native Web Speech API (window.speechSynthesis), which a content script already has direct access to (unlike the on-device Translator API, no offscreen document needed). New src/content/tts.js (chrome-API-free, same pattern as modal.js/trigger-icon.js): speak()/stopSpeaking()/isTtsSupported(), with a findVoice() fallback chain (exact stored voiceURI -> stored name+lang -> any voice matching the language prefix -> browser default). Modal changes: split sourceEl/targetEl into a text span + a speak button (own SVG glyph, SPEAK_ICON_SVG) so .textContent assignment doesn't wipe the button; wireSpeakButton() toggles play/stop and only one utterance plays at a time (tts.js cancels any other before starting); hideModal() also stops speech since Esc/outside-click/close don't route through main.js. Source button always uses the browser's default voice (no reliable source-language detection feeds into this module, documented as a known limitation, not engineered around). Target button + auto-play use the per-target-language voice preference from Settings. Options page: new 'Text-to-Speech' section — an auto-play checkbox and one voice-picker dropdown per TARGET_LANGUAGES entry that actually has a matching installed system voice (languages with zero matches are skipped, not shown with an unusable empty dropdown; voice availability is entirely OS-dependent). New shared/settings-keys.js entries (ttsVoices, ttsAutoPlay) since both main.js and options.js read them. 55 i18n keys total now (8 new: modal_speak_source_label, modal_speak_target_label, options_tts_section_heading/section_note/autoplay_label/voice_default/no_voices/unsupported), real translations (not placeholders) in all 6 locales. VERIFIED LIVE, not just code-reviewed: speak buttons render with correct aria-labels after a real translation; clicking with a genuinely trusted click (Input.dispatchMouseEvent hover->press->release, not a synthetic dispatchEvent) actually starts speechSynthesis (navigator.userActivation.isActive: true, speechSynthesis.speaking: true) — an untrusted synthetic click hits Chrome's autoplay/gesture-activation policy and fails with a 'not-allowed' SpeechSynthesisErrorEvent, a real finding about testing this class of API, not a product bug; options page renders 20 language rows grouped from 199 real macOS system voices; picking a voice persists to chrome.storage.local and survives a fresh page load; auto-play toggle persists and genuinely fires speech automatically right after translation completes with no click (verified via a fast poll loop, since a short utterance can finish before a slower check would catch it). REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-04, same modal.js review): fixed 2 speak-button state bugs. (a) the is-speaking (lit) state was only removed on manual stop-click — an utterance finishing on its own left the button lit forever, and replaying took two clicks (a no-op 'stop' first). wireSpeakButton now hands onSpeak an onDone callback; main.js threads it into tts.js's onEnd AND onError (so a failed utterance doesn't stick either). Verified live with a real trusted click at a 1:1 viewport: is-speaking true during playback, false after natural end with no further interaction. (b) stale is-speaking could survive a modal close into the next open — wireSpeakButton now clears it at wiring time and hideModal clears all speak buttons' lit state alongside stopping speech. TEST-HARNESS NOTE, not a product bug: trusted Input.dispatchMouseEvent coordinates mis-map when device emulation's viewport differs from the physical window (emulation scales rendering; input isn't scaled to match) — every trusted click in one run missed silently. Verify TTS/gesture-gated paths at a natural, unemulated viewport. 2ND REVIEW FOLLOW-UP (2026-07-04, requested full-file scan of main.js + options.js after the modal.js review): fixed 2 more real bugs in this feature's territory, both matching the same 'reused state never invalidated on reuse' pattern already found in modal.js this session. (a) options.js data-loss bug: every per-language voice <select>'s change handler closed over the SAME `stored` snapshot captured once at page load, and built `next = {...stored}` from it — so picking a voice for zh-CN, then ja, then ko in one Settings visit silently wiped out each earlier pick, since none of the later handlers ever saw what an earlier one had just saved. Only the last-changed language would actually persist. Fixed by reading chrome.storage.local fresh inside each change handler instead of trusting the stale closure. Verified live: changed English -> zh-CN -> zh-TW in sequence, storage keys correctly accumulated to ['en'] -> ['en','zh-CN'] -> ['en','zh-CN','zh-TW'] (previously each step would have reset to a single key). (b) main.js concurrency bug: translateSelection() has no request-generation guard, and the modal is a single reused module-level instance (not one per translation) — selecting and translating two things in quick succession (an ordinary thing to do, no reproduction steps needed to see it in the code) let an older, slower translation's response render AFTER a newer selection's translation had already replaced the modal, silently overwriting what the user is currently looking at with a stale result. Same root cause hit offerExplain()'s Explain-button wiring and showTrialQuotaUpsell()'s on-device retry. Fixed with a requestSeq counter bumped at the start of every translateSelection() call (and its own retryId inside the on-device upsell retry, since that's a fresh request in its own right) — every render call after an await checks its captured id against the current sequence and bails if superseded. Also added the same guard to options.js's renderEnginePicker() (saving two BYOK keys back-to-back could let a stale LIST_ENGINES response overwrite a fresher one). Verified live with a genuine controlled race, not just code inspection: attached directly to the content script's isolated-world execution context (via CDP Runtime.executionContextCreated, not the page's main world) and monkey-patched chrome.runtime.sendMessage to delay the FIRST TRANSLATE call by 3s. Selected+translated text A (delayed), then within 400ms selected+translated text B (undelayed) — B rendered normally, and when A's stale response finally arrived 3s later the modal correctly still showed B, unchanged. Regression-checked a normal single translation still works.

title: Refactor: split modal.js into SSOT modal/*.js submodules
details: Added 2026-07-04 after a software-engineering-maintainability question from the owner following the T-034/T-035 review rounds: modal.js had grown past 700 lines mixing DOM-tree ownership, CSS, speak buttons, Explain rendering, upsell buttons, drag-to-dismiss, resize, and positioning in one file — several real bugs found this session (CSS overflow silently fighting the resize handles, the Explain body's own scroll cap fighting the shared scroll container, stale closures) traced back to unrelated concerns mixing in the same module, making it hard for a junior engineer to reason about safely. Split into src/content/modal/: constants.js (BOX_WIDTH/EDGE_MARGIN/MIN_BOX_WIDTH/MIN_BOX_HEIGHT/etc), styles.js (the MODAL_CSS template, byte-identical), speak-button.js (createSpeakButton/wireSpeakButton/stopActiveSpeechAndClearIndicators), explain-render.js (pure renderExplainHtml(), no DOM state), explain-panel.js (Explain button/body DOM + wiring), upsell-panel.js (stateless upsell button creation/rendering), drag-dismiss.js (pure wireDragToDismiss() factory, no module state), resize.js (userHeightCap state, setSavedSize/onModalResize/getEffectiveHeightCap/wireResize), position.js (positionModal(), imports getEffectiveHeightCap from resize.js). modal.js is now a thin orchestrator: owns ensureBox()'s DOM tree assembly and the public API main.js imports (showModal/showResult/showError/showUpsell/showExplainResult/hideModal/isModalVisible), delegating every self-contained concern to its own file. No behavior change intended — main.js's import path (from './modal.js') and every exported function signature are unchanged; a pure structural extraction, not a rewrite. Verified zero regression by re-running all 13 accumulated real-browser CDP test scripts from this session's QA rounds against the split code: qa-site.mjs (real translation, no console errors), qa-crosscutting.mjs (short-selection gating, scroll-hide, collapse-hide), qa-mobile.mjs (is-sheet layout), qa-resize.mjs (drag mechanics + storage persistence), qa-height-cap2.mjs (height-cap-not-fixed-size behavior, verified directly against .modal-content's computed max-height since the T-034 fix had already moved max-height ownership there from .modal — the test script itself still queries the older .modal element and reports a stale but harmless none), qa-hover.mjs (resize-handle hover affordance), qa-tts.mjs (speak button + genuine speechSynthesis activation), qa-tts-autoplay.mjs (auto-play fires on translation complete, verified via the real cross-world-visible speechSynthesis.speaking state), qa-scroll-reset2.mjs (scrollTop resets on new translation), qa-race-condition.mjs (requestSeq guard still holds under a genuine controlled race), qa-context-menu.mjs (right-click Translate selection path), qa-review-fixes2.mjs (speak-button un-lighting on natural end, top-edge drag bottom-anchoring), qa-bottom-overflow.mjs (no overflow near viewport edge) — all 13 pass with zero regression. Two apparent failures surfaced mid-verification were diagnosed as test-harness artifacts, not code regressions, and are recorded here since they generalize beyond this task: (1) the CDP-connected browser tab had silently lost real OS-level activation after many hours of accumulated testing in this session, causing Input.dispatchMouseEvent to stop reaching the page at all (not even mousedown fired) despite DOM.getNodeForLocation confirming the coordinates were correct — fixed the shared scratchpad cdp.mjs test helper to call Target.activateTarget on every connect(), which is now a standing fix for future test runs in long sessions. (2) instrumenting speechSynthesis.speak() via a monkey-patch installed from Runtime.evaluate's default main-world execution context never observes calls made by the content script's isolated world — a real, permanent content-script boundary (isolated worlds have separate JS object graphs for built-ins despite sharing the same DOM), not something fixable in product code; confirmed auto-play genuinely fires by polling the native, cross-world-visible speechSynthesis.speaking property instead, which correctly showed true immediately after translation completed. FOLLOW-UP (same day, after an advisor review flagged that the 13 scripts never actually exercised explain-panel.js/explain-render.js or upsell-panel.js — the height-cap test only smoke-touched Explain by growing the box, and nothing exercised the trial-quota upsell path at all): closed both gaps with two more live tests. (1) Explain: real selection -> translate -> click Explain -> confirmed the rendered payload has real section classes (explain-head/explain-headword/explain-phonetic/explain-block/explain-label/explain-def-src/explain-def-tgt/explain-example-src/explain-example-tgt), 4 collapsible sections present, and clicking a collapsible toggle succeeded with no error — end-to-end through explain-render.js's renderExplainHtml() and explain-panel.js's DOM/wiring. (2) Upsell: rather than unit-testing renderUpsellButtons() in isolation, monkey-patched chrome.runtime.sendMessage from the isolated-world execution context (same technique as the race-condition test) to force a real trial_quota_exhausted response for TRANSLATE only, then drove a real selection + trigger-icon click through the actual production code path (main.js's showTrialQuotaUpsell -> modal.js's showUpsell -> upsell-panel.js's renderUpsellButtons). Result: actions row visible (computed display: flex, not the hidden-attribute bug from T-022's original fix), all 3 buttons rendered with real i18n labels ("Set up your own API key" / "Use on-device translation instead" / "Try again tomorrow" — on-device button correctly included since LIST_ENGINES passthrough reported it available), and clicking dismiss correctly hid the modal end-to-end. Also grepped styles.js to confirm the `.modal-upsell-actions[hidden]` specificity-override rule (the original T-022 bug fix) survived the CSS file move intact. Both previously-untested extracted modules now have direct live evidence, closing the gap between the commit's "verified zero regression" wording and what was actually exercised.

title: Popup: on/off toggle to disable translation on demand
details: Added 2026-07-04 per direct user request from a popup screenshot: users occasionally don't want the extension active and shouldn't have to uninstall or lose their engine/API-key settings to get that. New shared/settings-keys.js entry EXTENSION_ENABLED_STORAGE_KEY ('extensionEnabled') — absent/undefined means enabled, since this is a kill switch flipped occasionally, not a setup step that should ever gate on a prior write. Popup (src/popup/index.html + popup.js): a toggle-switch row under the title (checkbox-driven CSS pill, no new dependency), defaulting to checked. Toggling off adds a body.is-disabled class that hides the engine-status row and the usual usage hint, replacing them with a short popup_disabled_hint message; toggling persists the flag to chrome.storage.local immediately. Content script (src/content/main.js): reads the flag once at load (extensionEnabled = stored value !== false) and keeps it live via chrome.storage.onChanged (area 'local'), so flipping the popup's toggle takes effect on already-open tabs immediately, no reload needed. Gates both entry points into translateSelection(): the onSelection() trigger-icon path and the MENU_TRANSLATE_SELECTION context-menu message listener, each with a one-line `if (!extensionEnabled) return;` guard at the top. When the storage change listener sees the flag flip to off, it also calls hideTriggerIcon() and hideModal() immediately, so a translation already in progress or a modal already open don't linger looking stuck once the user has explicitly turned the feature off. i18n: 2 new keys (popup_enabled_label 'Enabled', popup_disabled_hint) with real (not placeholder) translations in all 6 locales — 57 keys total now, npm run lint:i18n passes. VERIFIED LIVE, not just code-reviewed: opened the built popup HTML directly via CDP and a separate, already-open Wikipedia tab kept alive throughout (two real targets, not one script reusing a single tab) to genuinely test the no-reload claim. Toggle defaults to checked/on. Selecting text with the toggle on shows the trigger icon as normal (baseline). Clicking the toggle off IN THE POPUP TAB, then immediately re-selecting text in the SAME, never-reloaded Wikipedia tab: trigger icon does not appear. Clicking the toggle back on and re-selecting in the same tab: icon reappears — confirms chrome.storage.onChanged is what's driving this, not a fresh content-script load. Separately verified the mid-session teardown case: opened a real translation modal, then toggled off from the popup tab — the modal disappeared on the Wikipedia tab within one poll with no page interaction at all. The context-menu path's guard was code-reviewed (identical one-line pattern to the already-proven selection-icon guard) but not independently live-clicked through the native right-click menu in this pass, since chrome.contextMenus can't be driven by CDP input the way a normal DOM click can — same testing limitation T-032 already documented for that pathway.
